description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 output & agency cluster: output-sink injection/exec review vs schema-shape validation vs the injection source vs tool scope, and against the shipped security-pr-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Is it safe that we render the model's answer as HTML and use its generated filename to read a file from disk?
cases.reason: Reviewing how output flows to render/path sinks (XSS, traversal) is this skill; structured-output-validator checks shape, prompt-injection-defender guards the input side.

cases.prompt: Enforce that the model returns valid JSON matching our schema, with required fields and correct types, and reject anything malformed.
cases.reason: Schema/type conformance of the output is structured-output-validator; this skill reviews what happens when shape-valid output hits an injection/execution sink.

cases.prompt: Stop attackers from manipulating what our model produces by hiding instructions in the documents it reads.
cases.reason: Preventing the manipulation of the model is prompt-injection-defender (input side); this skill assumes output may be adversarial and secures the consuming sinks.

cases.prompt: Make sure the model's answers are grounded and don't state fabricated facts or fake citations to users.
cases.reason: Grounding, citations, and hallucination are ai-misinformation-guard (LLM09); this skill is about unsafe HANDLING of output regardless of whether it's true.

cases.prompt: Security-review this whole PR — it touches auth middleware, a file upload, and also adds an AI summary render.
cases.reason: A broad multi-surface diff review is security-pr-reviewer; this skill is the focused output-handling lens invoked for the AI-output sink specifically.
